2026-04-20T17:43:58.1340179Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T17:43:58.1340727Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T17:43:58.1341531Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T17:43:58.1341900Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T17:43:58.1342215Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T17:43:58.1342454Z [36;1m[0m

Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-20T17:47:10.1352119Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T17:45:06.087836709Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58391,\"title\":\"[ResourceManager] Docs: Document AddArmClient IHostApplicationBuilder extensions\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58123 by `@m-nash`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58123 added four new public extension methods on `IHostApplicationBuilder` in a new file `ArmClientHostExtensions.cs`:\\n\\n- `AddArmClient(string sectionName)` — registers a singleton `ArmClient` from configuration\\n- `AddArmClient(string sectionName, Action configureSettings)` — same with settings callback\\n- `AddKeyedArmClient(string key, string sectionName)` — registers a keyed singleton `ArmClient`\\n- `AddKeyedArmClient(string key, string sectionName, Action configureSettings)` — same with settings callback\\n\\nThese are marked `[Experimental(\\u0026#34;SCME0002\\u0026#34;)]` and delegate to the generic `AddAzureClient` extensions from `System.ClientModel`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG** — `1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` has an empty `### Features Added` section; the new `AddArmClient`/`AddKeyedArmClient` APIs are not listed\\n- **README** — No section or example shows how to use `AddArmClient`/`AddKeyedArmClient` to register `ArmClient` via dependency injection in a .NET host application\\n- **No sample file** — There is no `tests/Samples/` file covering the `IHostApplicationBuilder` registration pattern, so there is nothing for the snippet tool to extract into the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit\\n\\nAdd under `## 1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` → `### Features Added`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- Added `AddArmClient` and `AddKeyedArmClient` extension methods on `IHostApplicationBuilder` to register `ArmClient` as a singleton via dependency injection. These methods are experimental (`SCME0002`) and delegate to `AddAzureClient` / `AddKeyedAzureClient`.\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nAdd a new subsection **\\u0026#34;Register with dependency injection\\u0026#34;** under the existing `## Getting started` → `### Authenticate the Client` section (or as a new top-level `## Dependency Injection` section near the Examples). Use a `C# Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient` placeholder that the snippet tool will populate:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Dependency Injection\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager` supports registering `ArmClient` in a .NET host application via `IHostApplicationBuilder`. This feature is experimental.\\n\\n### Register ArmClient\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient\\n```\\n\\n### Register a Keyed ArmClient\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddKeyedArmClient\\n```\\n````\\n\\n**File 3: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/tests/Samples/Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions.cs`** — Create\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Experimental(\\u0026#34;SCME0002\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void AddArmClientExample()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient\\n            // Call AddArmClient on an IHostApplicationBuilder to register ArmClient\\n            // as a singleton from the \\u0026#34;ArmClient\\u0026#34; configuration section.\\n            IHostApplicationBuilder builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder();\\n            builder.AddArmClient(\\u0026#34;ArmClient\\u0026#34;);\\n            IHost host = builder.Build();\\n            // Resolve ArmClient from DI\\n            ArmClient client = host.Services.GetRequiredService();\\n            #endregion\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            Assert.IsNotNull(client);\\n            host.Dispose();\\n#endif\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Experimental(\\u0026#34;SCME0002\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void AddKeyedArmClientExample()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddKeyedArmClient\\n            // Call AddKeyedArmClient to register a keyed ArmClient singleton.\\n            IHostApplicationBuilder builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder();\\n            builder.AddKeyedArmClient(\\u0026#34;myKey\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;ArmClient\\u0026#34;);\\n            IHost host = builder.Build();\\n            // Resolve the keyed ArmClient from DI\\n            ArmClient client = host.Services.GetRequiredKeyedService(\\u0026#34;myKey\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            Assert.IsNotNull(client);\\n            host.Dispose();\\n#endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet names are:\\n\\n| Snippet Name | Region in test file | README placeholder location |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient` | `Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions.AddArmClientExample` | Under new \\u0026#34;Register ArmClient\\u0026#34; subsection |\\n| `ArmClientHostExtensions_AddKeyedArmClient` | `Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions.AddKeyedArmClientExample` | Under new \\u0026#34;Register a Keyed ArmClient\\u0026#34; subsection |\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nCurrent README sections in order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate the Client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ❌ **Missing:** Dependency Injection / `AddArmClient` registration example\\n5. ✅ (Troubleshooting / Next steps / Contributing — verify these exist and are complete)\\n\\nAdd the new \\u0026#34;Dependency Injection\\u0026#34; section (with snippet placeholders) as described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcemanager` — confirms README placeholders are populated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcemanager` — no public API changes expected (API was already exported in PR #58123), but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24680897657/agentic_workflow) · ● 520.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58391\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T17:42:01Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T17:42:01Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

- `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-20T17:47:10.1486721Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T17:45:18.632251904Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58391,\"title\":\"[ResourceManager] Docs: Document AddArmClient IHostApplicationBuilder extensions\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58123 by `@m-nash`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58123 added four new public extension methods on `IHostApplicationBuilder` in a new file `ArmClientHostExtensions.cs`:\\n\\n- `AddArmClient(string sectionName)` — registers a singleton `ArmClient` from configuration\\n- `AddArmClient(string sectionName, Action configureSettings)` — same with settings callback\\n- `AddKeyedArmClient(string key, string sectionName)` — registers a keyed singleton `ArmClient`\\n- `AddKeyedArmClient(string key, string sectionName, Action configureSettings)` — same with settings callback\\n\\nThese are marked `[Experimental(\\u0026#34;SCME0002\\u0026#34;)]` and delegate to the generic `AddAzureClient` extensions from `System.ClientModel`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG** — `1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` has an empty `### Features Added` section; the new `AddArmClient`/`AddKeyedArmClient` APIs are not listed\\n- **README** — No section or example shows how to use `AddArmClient`/`AddKeyedArmClient` to register `ArmClient` via dependency injection in a .NET host application\\n- **No sample file** — There is no `tests/Samples/` file covering the `IHostApplicationBuilder` registration pattern, so there is nothing for the snippet tool to extract into the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit\\n\\nAdd under `## 1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` → `### Features Added`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- Added `AddArmClient` and `AddKeyedArmClient` extension methods on `IHostApplicationBuilder` to register `ArmClient` as a singleton via dependency injection. These methods are experimental (`SCME0002`) and delegate to `AddAzureClient` / `AddKeyedAzureClient`.\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nAdd a new subsection **\\u0026#34;Register with dependency injection\\u0026#34;** under the existing `## Getting started` → `### Authenticate the Client` section (or as a new top-level `## Dependency Injection` section near the Examples). Use a `C# Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient` placeholder that the snippet tool will populate:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Dependency Injection\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager` supports registering `ArmClient` in a .NET host application via `IHostApplicationBuilder`. This feature is experimental.\\n\\n### Register ArmClient\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient\\n```\\n\\n### Register a Keyed ArmClient\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddKeyedArmClient\\n```\\n````\\n\\n**File 3: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/tests/Samples/Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions.cs`** — Create\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Experimental(\\u0026#34;SCME0002\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void AddArmClientExample()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient\\n            // Call AddArmClient on an IHostApplicationBuilder to register ArmClient\\n            // as a singleton from the \\u0026#34;ArmClient\\u0026#34; configuration section.\\n            IHostApplicationBuilder builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder();\\n            builder.AddArmClient(\\u0026#34;ArmClient\\u0026#34;);\\n            IHost host = builder.Build();\\n            // Resolve ArmClient from DI\\n            ArmClient client = host.Services.GetRequiredService();\\n            #endregion\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            Assert.IsNotNull(client);\\n            host.Dispose();\\n#endif\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Experimental(\\u0026#34;SCME0002\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void AddKeyedArmClientExample()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddKeyedArmClient\\n            // Call AddKeyedArmClient to register a keyed ArmClient singleton.\\n            IHostApplicationBuilder builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder();\\n            builder.AddKeyedArmClient(\\u0026#34;myKey\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;ArmClient\\u0026#34;);\\n            IHost host = builder.Build();\\n            // Resolve the keyed ArmClient from DI\\n            ArmClient client = host.Services.GetRequiredKeyedService(\\u0026#34;myKey\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            Assert.IsNotNull(client);\\n            host.Dispose();\\n#endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet names are:\\n\\n| Snippet Name | Region in test file | README placeholder location |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient` | `Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions.AddArmClientExample` | Under new \\u0026#34;Register ArmClient\\u0026#34; subsection |\\n| `ArmClientHostExtensions_AddKeyedArmClient` | `Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions.AddKeyedArmClientExample` | Under new \\u0026#34;Register a Keyed ArmClient\\u0026#34; subsection |\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nCurrent README sections in order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate the Client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ❌ **Missing:** Dependency Injection / `AddArmClient` registration example\\n5. ✅ (Troubleshooting / Next steps / Contributing — verify these exist and are complete)\\n\\nAdd the new \\u0026#34;Dependency Injection\\u0026#34; section (with snippet placeholders) as described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order.
